executive_summary.narrative: Both backend and frontend container images carry critical and high-severity CVEs in their base-image OS packages (OpenSSL, Node.js runtime, zlib). These are container-layer findings, distinct from application dependency findings.

top_risks.title: Critical OpenSSL CVE in backend base image
top_risks.impact: A critical, widely-exploited class of vulnerability in the TLS library.
top_risks.why_it_matters: OpenSSL CVEs are frequently weaponized quickly after disclosure.
top_risks.recommended_action: Rebuild on an updated base image.

top_risks.title: Critical Node.js runtime CVE in frontend base image
top_risks.impact: Critical CVE in the JS runtime itself, not application code.
top_risks.why_it_matters: Affects the entire frontend container regardless of application-level fixes.
top_risks.recommended_action: Rebuild on an updated Node base image.

priority_actions.title: Rebuild both images on current base-image tags
priority_actions.rationale: Both critical findings and most of the high findings are resolved by a base-image bump, not application code changes.
priority_actions.expected_risk_reduction: Resolves all 5 container-layer CVEs in one CI change.

release_readiness.rationale: Critical container-layer CVEs exist, but the fix (a base-image rebuild) is low-complexity and well-understood — a human should confirm whether to block on this or fast-track the rebuild.
